quick_facts.label: Entry examination
quick_facts.value: For central-government LDC/JSA recruitment, SSC conducts the Combined Higher Secondary (10+2) Level (CHSL) Examination. SSC’s 2026–27 tentative calendar lists CHSL 2026, with notification planned for April 2026, closing in May 2026, and Tier-I tentatively scheduled for July–September 2026; candidates should rely on the applicable notification for final dates and conditions.

quick_facts.label: Minimum education
quick_facts.value: In the SSC CHSL 2025 notice, LDC/JSA required a pass in 12th Standard (or equivalent) from a recognized board or university. Requirements and cut-off dates are notification-specific.

quick_facts.label: Post classification and placement
quick_facts.value: SSC’s CHSL 2025 notice described LDC/JSA as Group C posts recruited for ministries, departments and offices of the Government of India, as well as constitutional/statutory bodies and tribunals reporting vacancies.

quick_facts.label: Typing requirement
quick_facts.value: The CHSL 2025 LDC/JSA typing test was qualifying in nature: candidates chose Hindi or English; the prescribed speeds were 30 w.p.m. in Hindi and 35 w.p.m. in English, assessed for accuracy on a computer-based 10-minute passage.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: The SSC CHSL 2025 notice placed LDC/JSA at Central Pay Matrix Level 2, ₹19,900–₹63,200. This is the published pay-level range, not a guaranteed take-home salary; actual monthly pay varies with admissible allowances, place of posting, deductions and later government revisions.

sections.heading: What an LDC/JSA does
paragraphs: LDC/JSA is a routine clerical and records-support role. Official duty manuals describe work such as receiving and registering dak (official mail), maintaining section, file and file-movement registers, indexing and recording, typing and comparing correspondence, dispatch, preparing routine statements, and putting up simple drafts.

The precise assignment differs by office. For example, an academy-level government duty statement includes maintenance of files, registration of papers, circulars/registers, records management, typing, diary/dispatch and administrative support; therefore, the role should be understood as office- and section-dependent rather than a single identical job description everywhere.
bullets: Maintain orderly file, diary, dispatch and record workflows.
Prepare and type routine official correspondence and statements.
Track incoming/outgoing communications and assist the section’s administrative work.
Carry out other clerical tasks assigned by supervisors under the office’s procedures.

sections.heading: Main route: SSC CHSL
paragraphs: The standard direct-entry route is SSC CHSL. The SSC CHSL 2025 notice covered recruitment to LDC/JSA across reporting central-government user departments. It required 12th Standard/equivalent for LDC/JSA, subject to the notice’s recognition, documentation, age and other eligibility provisions.

Selection under the cited CHSL notice involved a computer-based Tier-I examination and Tier-II modules, including mathematical abilities, reasoning/general intelligence, English, general awareness, computer knowledge and a qualifying LDC/JSA typing test. Candidates must check the notification for their own cycle because schedules, vacancies, age cut-offs, reservation, post preferences and department-specific conditions can change.

A bachelor’s degree—such as BA, B.Com or a recognized distance-learning degree—is not the baseline qualification stated for LDC/JSA in the cited CHSL notice, but it can support later education or career choices. SSC notes that open/distance credentials used for eligibility must meet the applicable recognition requirements.
bullets: Complete Class 12/equivalent from a recognized institution.
Apply through the SSC portal when the relevant CHSL notification opens.
Prepare for Tier-I and Tier-II computer-based modules.
Build Hindi or English typing speed and accuracy for the qualifying typing test.
Submit post/department preferences and complete document verification when instructed.

sections.heading: Skills that matter
paragraphs: Typing accuracy in Hindi or English is a formal entry skill for the SSC LDC/JSA route. The 2025 CHSL scheme also tests computer knowledge, including computer basics, Microsoft Office fundamentals, internet/e-mail use, and basic networking and cybersecurity concepts.

On the job, the documented work makes careful records handling, written communication, document tracking, routine drafting, filing discipline and familiarity with office procedures especially relevant. Because clerks work with official correspondence and files, accuracy and orderly follow-through are as important as speed.
bullets: Hindi or English keyboarding with accuracy.
Word processing, spreadsheets and basic presentation software.
Email, web and file-management fundamentals.
Record keeping, indexing, diary/dispatch and document tracking.
Clear routine written communication and attention to detail.

sections.heading: Where the role is performed
paragraphs: SSC recruits LDC/JSA for vacancies reported by a range of central-government ministries, departments and offices, and also by constitutional bodies, statutory bodies and tribunals. Accordingly, the employing organization—not SSC—ultimately determines the particular office, section, work allocation and local conditions after selection.

The work is principally office-based clerical support, centered on correspondence, registers, files and records. Some user departments may attach additional post-specific requirements: the CHSL 2025 notice, for example, separately flagged physical and medical standards for Lower Division Clerk vacancies in the Border Roads Organisation.
bullets: Central ministry, department or attached/subordinate office.
Constitutional or statutory body, or tribunal, where vacancies are reported.
Section/administrative office dealing with correspondence, records and routine establishment work.
Potentially department-specific conditions for particular allocations.

sections.heading: Progression and departmental pathways
paragraphs: Progression is cadre- and department-specific, so an LDC/JSA should consult the recruitment rules and promotion orders of the employing organization rather than assume one universal ladder. In the Central Secretariat Clerical Service (CSCS), DoPT’s 2024–25 annual report records promotions of Junior Secretariat Assistants to Senior Secretariat Assistant under seniority and departmental-examination quota, demonstrating an established JSA-to-SSA route in that cadre.

Departmental competitive examinations can be relevant within particular cadres. SSC’s 2026–27 calendar separately lists JSA/LDC Grade and SSA/UDC Grade Limited Departmental Competitive Examinations for DoPT, while the DoPT report records promotions at JSA and SSA levels. A degree can be pursued alongside service, but it does not by itself guarantee promotion; applicable service rules, vacancy position, seniority/selection process and qualifying conditions govern advancement.
bullets: Check the employer’s cadre-specific recruitment rules and promotion orders.
In CSCS, a documented next grade is Senior Secretariat Assistant (SSA).
Track eligible limited departmental competitive examinations where applicable.
Use further study—such as BA, B.Com or recognized distance learning—to broaden future options, while verifying recognition and service-rule requirements.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As published in SSC’s CHSL 2025 notice, LDC/JSA carries Pay Matrix Level 2, with a stated range of ₹19,900–₹63,200. This is useful as a dated central-pay reference point, but it should not be read as a current in-hand-pay quotation.

The amount actually received depends on factors including the employee’s point in the pay matrix, admissible central-government allowances, posting location and statutory or other deductions. Applicants should use the latest recruitment notice and the Department of Expenditure’s current pay/allowance orders when evaluating compensation.
bullets: Published CHSL 2025 pay level: Level 2.
Published Level 2 range in that notice: ₹19,900–₹63,200.
Take-home pay is variable rather than fixed by the quoted range alone.
